strategy: Buy $13 Call — Sep 2026 (enter AFTER May 7 earnings)

wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering new CSP positions.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: Daily chart, 20-period 2σ Bollinger Bands. Lower BB estimated ~$9.50–11.00 given elevated volatility. Wait for price to tag or breach lower band. Current price $12.11 is mid-to-upper band.

csp.annualisedYield: ~40–75% annualised on capital at risk (high IV small cap)
csp.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured. CRITICAL: Close before May 7 earnings — IV crush risk. Re-enter after.
csp.note: Small cap nuclear modular reactor. IV likely ~80–110%. Low capital at risk ($900–$1,100/contract). Size to 3–5 contracts. ⚠️ Earnings May 7 — do NOT hold CSP through earnings.

coveredCall.exitRule: Close at 50% premium. Re-sell monthly. Close before earnings.
coveredCall.note: If assigned (cost basis ~$9–11), sell CC at $13–14 to earn premium while stock recovers toward analyst targets.

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter after May 7 earnings IV crush settles. Stock at $13.29 — slightly above $13 strike (near-ATM). Best entry on a post-earnings dip toward $11.50–12.50.
exitRules.profitExit: Close at 50–70% gain on premium. Target $16–17 for first exit (~25–30% upside), ride toward $20+ analyst target for 100%+ gain.
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not cleared $15 by ~June 25 (50% of time elapsed on Sep position), take profits or close. Do not hold a decaying near-ATM call past mid-point.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 40–50% loss. SMR is a volatile small-cap — gaps are common in either direction.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close by August 2 (5 days before ~August 7 Q2 earnings). High IV at 62% means IV crush will be severe if held through earnings.

options.bestFor: Avoid — only 13 days left at the August 2 close. Theta destroys most value in the final 2 weeks. Almost no time to benefit from any move.

Recommended — 48 days of remaining time value at the August 2 close. Covers the full pre-earnings window with enough time for the SMR bull thesis to develop. IV at 62% warrants monitoring for spread entry if premium exceeds $4.
Provides two earnings cycles of coverage. Better suited for a long-term LEAPS approach — overkill for a near-term catalyst play. Sep 19 is better value.

recommendation.title: Near-ATM Call — Enter POST-Earnings (May 7)
recommendation.entryNote: WAIT until after May 7 earnings — IV is extremely elevated and will crush post-announcement regardless of direction. The day after earnings is ideal entry. High short interest (23.9% monthly increase) creates violent squeeze potential on any positive catalyst. Target the $13 strike: deep OTM pays poorly at $20 target, ITM gives you less leverage per dollar.

companyOverview: NuScale Power is the only company with an NRC-certified Small Modular Reactor (SMR) design — a regulatory moat that took 9 years and $1B+ to achieve. Their SMR is 12x smaller than a conventional nuclear plant, designed for modular deployment with dramatically lower upfront capital costs. Despite the unique technology position, NuScale has no signed commercial customers and is pre-revenue at scale. The stock crashed from $57 (mid-2025) to $8.85 (late 2025) on a cascade of bad news: the Carbon-Free Power Project (CFPP) anchor deal collapsed in November 2023 after cost estimates ballooned from $58/MWh to $89+/MWh, strategic backer Fluor sold its entire ~126M share position ($2.43B in proceeds) over Oct 2025–Apr 2026, and cash burn accelerated 323% YoY. The stock has since rebounded to $12.11 on the February 2026 Romania Doicesti Final Investment Decision and the White House space nuclear initiative — but remains 79% below peak. Key thesis: NuScale is the only NRC-certified SMR vendor. If AI-driven power demand forces data center operators to commit to SMR deployments in 2026–2027, NuScale is the only game in town. First commercial customer signing would be a watershed event.
catalysts: Romania Doicești FID (Feb 2026) — $4.9B first commercial SMR deployment approved, commercial operation 2030–2032; proof-of-concept for the business model
Only NRC-certified SMR — 9-year, $1B+ regulatory moat; X-energy, Oklo, Kairos, TerraPower still years from certification
White House space nuclear initiative — government contracts targeted specifically at NRC-certified designs
ENTRA1 Energy $25B partnership framework — largest SMR offtake framework ever announced (still non-binding)
Amazon SMR industry validation — tech hyperscalers buying nuclear power drives mainstream customer awareness
AI data center power demand — cloud giants need 24/7 carbon-free baseload that solar/wind cannot provide; SMRs are structurally positioned
Fluor exit complete — ~126M shares overhang now gone; forced seller removed, float normalizing
Short squeeze potential — 23.9% monthly short interest increase; first customer contract could trigger violent covering
DOE loan guarantee support — federal backing reduces financing risk for first-mover customers
First commercial customer signing is the watershed event — would re-rate the stock by 40–80% in one day
risks: May 7 earnings: enter AFTER — IV crush will happen regardless of beat/miss; enter the day after
No signed commercial customers — multiple missed internal deadlines; 'hard contracts by end-2025' guidance was missed
Cash burn: $459.6M in 2025 vs $108.7M in 2024 (323% increase) — equity raise likely if revenue misses 2027 targets
Revenue ramp is distant — Romania Doicești commercial operation not until 2030–2032; investors wait 4–6 years
Securities class action filed April 2026 — alleges misleading statements about CFPP viability; legal overhang and costs
CFPP collapse history — cost estimates ballooned from $58/MWh to $89+/MWh; cost discipline track record is poor
Competition intensifying: X-energy (Amazon-backed), Oklo (Sam Altman-backed), Kairos (Google), TerraPower (Bill Gates) — all racing for NRC certification
Technology risk — no full-scale commercial SMR has ever been built; engineering surprises are possible
Regulatory approval in new markets requires country-by-country NRC equivalent certification — long tail

dilution.summary: The Fluor overhang is gone. Future dilution depends on whether revenue materializes before 2027 cash cliff.

items.label: Fluor exit — COMPLETE (Oct 2025–Apr 2026)
items.detail: Strategic backer sold entire ~126M share stake for $2.43B in proceeds. This was the primary driver of the $57→$8.85 crash. Overhang is now fully removed — a meaningful positive going forward.

items.label: Current cash runway (through 2027)
items.detail: $1.3B cash with $170–200M guided 2026 OpEx. If Romania milestones slip and no revenue materializes, equity raise at depressed prices becomes likely in late 2027.

items.label: 2025 burn acceleration risk
items.detail: $459.6M burned in 2025 vs $108.7M in 2024 (+323%). Company guided lower 2026 OpEx but has missed cost targets before. Watch quarterly cash burn updates closely.

items.label: Securities class action (Apr 2026)
items.detail: Class action filed alleging misleading statements about CFPP commercial viability. Settlement or judgment could require additional cash outlay; risk to capital position.
